body_txt: Makeup has been around for a very long time; not just to hide imperfections, but for ritual and religious purposes as well. Depending on the time and place, wearing it was also not an especially gendered practice: In Ancient Egypt , both high-class men and women would wear eyeliner and nail polish to protect themselves against curses, as well as more mundane things like biting insects and the glare of the Sun. In the court of Versailles, everyone wore thick layers of make-up and doused themselves in perfume (partly as a status symbol, but mostly to hide the smell of rarely-washed bodies). Nowadays, makeup is largely considered a strictly feminine accoutrement, and men typically wear it only for cultural reasons, as a subversive fashion statement, or if they're appearing on stage (and the last two can often overlap). In fiction, makeup on men can indicate a religious figure or project a general air of mystery. In cases of Makeup Is Evil , it is worn by insane psychopathic clowns , people with a screw loose, or - in tamer cases - the more emo-tastic brand of Bad Boys . It also acts as a fetish; just ask any fan of Captain Jack Sparrow . In recent decades, it notably gained a reputation as part of the look in Emo and Goth subcultures, among others. Leaves one prone to Your Makeup Is Running . See also Excessive Evil Eyeshadow and Real Men Wear Pink . Compare and contrast Crossdresser , Camp Gay , Camp Straight , and Paper-Thin Disguise .
